title: CGAP Human Spleen Dissociation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"36dr0","text":"Mechanical dissociation of human spleen tissue to single cells for use in 10X chromium scRNA-Seq preparation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Receive spleen punch in solution.
Place spleen punch onto 100mm Glass Petri Dish.
Add 10ml Cold PBS.
Slice spleen punch into small pieces (approximately 10x10x10mm).
Transfer spleen pieces and PBS into 50ml Falcon Tube.
Add 100µl DNAse I dropwise to spleen punch in PBS to a final concentration of 100µg/ml.
Incubate at room temperature for 5 min.
Mash spleen through 100µM cell strainer above a 50ml Falcon Tube using a 2.0ml syringe plunger, washing through with 10ml Cold PBS.
Transfer to 15ml Falcon Tube.
Centrifuge at 500g for 5 min at 4°C.
Add 1ml Red Cell Lysis buffer to the pellet and resuspend by racking/tapping.
Place on ice 5 min with periodic agitation.
Add 9ml Cold PBS.
Centrifuge at 500g for 5 min at 4°C.
Resuspend pellet in 5ml Cold PBS with 0.04% BSA (8µl BSA/ml PBS).
Filter through a 100µM cell strainer above a 50ml Falcon Tube.
Wash cell strainer through with 5 ml Cold PBS with 0.04% BSA (8µl BSA/ml PBS).
Count cells using trypan blue and a C-Chip or the nucleocounter.

If percentage of live cells is higher than 70-80%, cells can then be processed for scRNA-seq.a. If percentage of live cells is below 70-80%, remove dead cells by following “MACS Live Dead Separation”. CGAP MACS Live Dead Separation by Adam Hunter
Dilute cells to 2x10 6 cell per ml in 0.04% BSA and proceed to 10X preparation for scRNA sequencing.

Ensure all unused tissue, equipment and tubes that have been in contact with primary tissue are placed into Virkon in sweetie jar for a minimum of 1 hour. After this time aspirate and disposing in relevant sharps or waste routes.
Perform Freezing Human Tissue in Isopentane before beginning Dissociation Protocol
